_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in conducting forensic analyses in multiple operating system environments (e.g., mobile device systems).
reference_document_element_description: Skill in operating a data catalog platform, including configuring connectors, reviewing harvested metadata, curating business context, and managing access.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI DQ domain. DQ-S-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-S-012 (TF, cosine: 0.499), SP-S-008 (SP, cosine: 0.485).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.8/10 (raw 0.381). Shared vocabulary: [operating, skill]. DCWF-only terms [analyses, conducting, device, environments, forensic, mobile] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, business, catalog, configuring, connectors, context] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.868 (P=0.869, R=0.866). Balanced P/R — the 14-word DCWF element and 19-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (99.8%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Skill in conducting forensic analyses in multiple operating system environments" and "Skill in operating a data catalog platform, including configuring connectors, re". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.506 > STS 0.381 (gap +0.125). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [operating, skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.0%. Shared tokens: [operating, skill]. DCWF-only: [analyses, conducting, device, environments, forensic, mobile]. WIDAI-only: [access, business, catalog, configuring, connectors, context].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 99.8% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.125 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
